review_text: Ahead of its time many movies want to be a sci fi noir type movie but always fail to deliver but this movie does it right and no movie will ever do it the way this movie did .
Best with some Jim beam.

Un regalo da parte di questo film è la favolosa interpretazione di Harrison Ford che in un film tutt'altro che semplice dà il massimo e riesce ad immedesimarsi alla perfezione in un personaggio disilluso e solo, che alla luce che talvolta illumina la sua casa riflette profondamente, seduto su una poltrona. Certo Blade runner è anche un bellissimo film per chi cerca azione, inseguimenti e scene anche un po' violente. Gli inseguimenti sotto la pioggia, tra la gente, con il protagonista che mira al bersaglio cercando di evitare la moltitudine di gente che passeggia per quella sporca Los Angeles. Blade runner è una parabola sull'uomo e l'eternità, l'uomo che cerca il suo creatore, che cerca una motivo per cui tutto ciò che ha vissuto deve andare perduto, perché non vuole che questo succeda. Il protagonista con i suoi occhi vedrà tutto ciò capendo il significato. Blade runner è il miglior film di Ridley Scott, senza dubbio, un autentico capolavoro

Great to see this classic having a rerun on the big IMAX screen 44 years after its initial release. Sure 2019 didn’t quite reach those dizzy heights of space colonies and near perfect robots, but what with the constant rain in LA and everyone still smoking like chimneys? However the Coca Cola and Budweiser product placements were spot on! But seriously this was a great sci-fi movie for its time. Hugely enjoyable to watch again all these decades later. The Vangelis soundtrack has stood the test of time and the special effects leave some more recent ones for dead. Harrison Ford and Rutger Hauer have stellar performances. Wonderful memories …
One of The Best Cyberpunk Movies !!!
Beyond what may be coming to humans, the author saw things that I could not see.
One of the best film adaptions of a Philip K. Dick book ever made.
Blade runner is a good movie it has as a great story line, it has great actors, it has very good action
Slow and too many plot holes.
In terms of Atmospherics, nothing can beat this movie. A dark, wet and compelling future megacity. In terms of story, a masterpiece by a master of Sci-Fi.
This is one movie I have watched over and over and over again. It's fantastic.
Yup, just like when it first came out, no one liked it. Including me. It's boring, and hard to understand. MAYBE, JUST MAYBE I'll watch it again.
A brilliant film with a fantastic aesthetic. For 1982, they did a wonderful job portraying the future dystopia.

Saw it aged 13 when it came out and didn't like it. Rewatched it 43 years later and still didn't. Visually it's very impressive but it reminded me of an extended pop video on a huge budget. I think my biggest problem is that I felt totally unengaged with the film's premise and characters. To whom am I supposed to relate and for what purpose? So many viewers cite this as a masterpiece but even after a four decade gap I found it a classic exercise of style over substance.
It bored the hell outa me when it came out and 43 years later it’s still slow.

The music makes the film as much as the story. I can watch it again and again just for the music. It's perfect if you want a slow relaxing sci-fi not a blazing fast shoot out. The let downs is that there are a few holes in the plot like how many replicants reached earth? I also pity Scott had to predict 2019 - the fashion, the social scenes, the technology, the climate. Because obviously now we can mock this whereas in the 80's we wouldn't have. So you have to allow for this and not judge it. Bladerunner 2049 will also age! There's also a few disturbing scenes with eyes being pushed in and where Deckard 'forces himself' onto Rachel. But apart from those issues the characters are superb and unique. Very well cast.

Probably my favourite film, and in terms of how it looks and the special effects, it still holds up decades later. Soundtrack is fantastic. Definitely prefer the version without the voice over as i don't think anything needs explaining in this film. Brilliant!!
Brilliant, original, thought-provoking. Wonderful cast, great movie. Probably Ridley Scott's best movie.

Roy Batty: "I've seen things you people wouldn't believe. Attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion. I watched c-beams glitter in the dark near the Tannhäuser Gate. All those moments will be lost in time, like tears in rain. Time to die."

I liked the vibe. The fight scenes were bad acting and the storyline was so confusing, it didn't make watching a great experience. Overall not bad, but also not good

I have to agree with those who think this movie is over-rated. Naturally the story has philosophical pretentions - Sci-Fi tends to. I've watched it about 4 times, and every single time I've fallen asleep during it. Yes, pathos is achieved in Hauer's death. Until then, it's slow and predictable.
A masterpiece that gets better with every viewing and stands the test of time.
I can appreciate the impact of this film at the time of release but I don't find it to my tastes and the editing is quite dated now. It definitely has a charm to it still though.
It's a masterpiece. Atmospheric and sad. Not much to say about this classic that hasn't already been said. There aren't many movies that I can watch multiple times, but this is one of those.
Scotts the science fiction master! A unique and beautiful film. One of a kind.

Admittedly, I'm 40 years late in watching this movie, but I was unimpressed. The script left a lot to be desired, Harrison's acting is wooden and almost painful at times. Daryll Hannah's death scene was disturbing and I don't need to watch this again. I'm sure it was cutting edge at the time of release, but it doesn't hold up decades later.

It’s well done and on paper I should love it going by it’s attributes (a classic 80s sci fi, the fact Harrison Ford is in it, etc). But I just find it too sleepy and dull for me, I feel this one is just a warm up to the brilliance of Blade Runner 2049 which is superior in every way to this one. Watch Harrison Ford as Han Solo in the first 3 Star Wars for a better sci fi ride.

I could see how this could have been something great, but I honestly think this film dropped the ball. This feels like it’s missing key scenes that pull this movie together. The scene-to-scene story is choppy and seriously confusing. The story and the meaning this film went for were interesting, and if done better, would have been a fun watch.
A true classic--Groundbreaking in so many respects. The special effects may be a bit dated but still dead-on. Directness or subtlety--It has it all. See it again and again.

One of the worst movies I’ve ever seen. I don’t even really know what the point of this movie is. It was simply just boring. Nothing really happened. He killed a few people and then the movie ended.

60% rating ... Pretty solid movie with an interesting plot. Biggest complaint is easily the incredibly slow and painful pacing. The film seemed like it kept dragging to the point of borderline boredom.

This film is sublime from start to finish. An amazing cast, great cinematography, perfect soundtrack, thought provoking plot and symbolism, and a beautifully written script. The film does not falter in any aspect and is a cult classic for a reason. If you haven't watched it, you must. It will leave you questioning reality and life. A 10/10 must watch.

I find it incredibly beautiful. From the moment it starts with those deep notes as the opening credits start to roll. So many scenes are set up like a work of art almost, for example the first time Dekard meets Rachel at the Tyrell Corporation.
If you're a Fan of Phillip K Dick, it's the perfect accompaniment to the book ,plus it's probably the best sci fi since Kubrick 2001

Really bad. Confusing. Slow. Boring, It came out when I was seven years old and for the last few decades, I tried to watch it 5-6 different times and could never get through it all. Acting in it is not particularly good either with the exception of Harrison Ford and Rutger.. The high score on here is mind-boggling.

This is Ridley Scott's finest hour. This is a sci-fi noir masterpiece from the beginning to the end. It's not just the atmosphere. It's the excellent acting. It's the incredible soundtrack from the legendary Vangelis (how can you write a review and not mention this) and the plot. YES, the plot. It's unbelievable so many people (kids mostly) can't grasp what is goin on. This movie is 42 years old and we STILL ask each other if Harrison Ford was human or not, we still argue about so many things. This is not a movie you watch eating burgers, playing with your cellphone and talking to people. It needs your attention, it needs your sense of art, if you have one. This is a timeless masterpiece, this is food for thought even in 2025. What makes a human, what is a machine? Scott can't make a good historic movie, but when it comes to sci-fi, he is a grandmaster.

One of the best movies, and probably the best sci-fi movie ever made. The visuals are stunning, and as they were created using photographic techniques, they appear more lifelike than the sterile CGI landscapes we see now. As for the rare, low scoring reviews here, they either betray a negative reaction to the dystopian future envisioned in Philip K. Dick's novel, or difficulty following the minimalist plot of a detective (a "Blade Runner") charged with tracking down and killing a group of escaped "replicant" slaves. Mercifully, the director (Ridley Scott) and script writers didn't dumb things down to appeal to the "Star Wars" conditioned audiences of the time, otherwise this film would probably never have been remembered. This is a hand made film created by a group of the most uniquely talented artists available who were allowed to develop a vision of Dick's story without too much interference from their financiers. It's the kind of thing that will probably never be seen again given the advent of AI, CGI, and oversight by corporate committee.

The story delves into the film noir genre with a hint of romance. The visuals are breathtaking and have stood the test of time. The standout feature of this film is the incredible set design and landscapes. Ridley Scott makes excellent use of colors, uniquely depicting the futuristic city of Los Angeles as both gloomy and sparkling by blending dark tones with touches of bright colors. Even though the world of Blade Runner has been an inspiration for many artists, the film is not perfect. It tries to mix the story of the hunt for the fugitive Replicants with a romantic arc between Deckard and a Replicant from Tyrell, which could have been wiped off the script for the better. Overall, the film features superb visuals and good performances, but also a terrible romantic arc and questionable character choices. Even though I think the film is very good, I wish the plot had been more polished. Read more on my Substack: https://lennycritiques.substack.com/p/blade-runner-1982-b1b

Filme mais ou menos, o roteiro é fraco, as cenas são mais ou menos, a história é mais ou menos, o elenco é mais ou menos, e alguns ajudam a melhorar o filme, os personagens são fracos, e o filme deveria ter cenas bem melhores e relevantes, para ser bom.

Visually great. The sets are amazing. Makes you think about how far into the future replicants could definitely happen, considering they can already do it with sheep. Very contemplative and great theme. Overall great movie. 9.9/10.

This film was incredible on so many levels. The story was compelling and the visuals were beautiful, and the scenery and atmosphere the movie produced were astounding. I had so much fun watching this movie, I definitely recommend it for sci-fi fans!

It's good but not great. I,robot is a better movie. I liked the characters but the story was simple and not that exciting. At times I was bored. Overall it's a good movie but hopefully the sequel is better. Tim Treakle

Just might be the most iconic sci-fi film ever made. Way ahead of it's time. The menacing vibe provided by the production and cinematography of those rain slicked streets and seedy vistas will not be replicated anytime soon. Eerie is the word. Ridley Scott have a winner here and the character cast is spot on. Masterpiece
The dark, dystopian feel of this film, combined with the music by Vangelis makes this a masterpiece.
